preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the neutral schwa [ə] is a common form of L-vocalization and vowel reduction. It occurs when the 'dark L' [ɫ] loses its primary oral contact, leaving behind a centralized vocalic residue, typically in unstressed or syllable-final positions.

examples.note: Syllabic L vocalizes to a neutral schwa.
Coda L vocalizes into a central off-glide or schwa quality.
